axis: blacklist TTL + RAM threshold + queue deadlines — fixed-policy adaptivity

session_evidence_2026_04_26.log: hexa_remote: host=ubu1 SSH OK but RAM avail=23934MB < 24576MB threshold (raw#36 bracket) — skipping
session_evidence_2026_04_26.interpretation: ubu1 had 23934MB free; threshold is 24576MB; gap = 642MB (2.6%). Run rejected. No graceful retry with smaller workload.

session_evidence_2026_04_26.log: hexa_remote: host=ubu1 blacklisted 64s ago (<300s TTL) — skipping probe
session_evidence_2026_04_26.interpretation: after one rejection, ubu1 frozen for 5min. Even if RAM clears in 30s, 5min lockout.

session_evidence_2026_04_26.log: hexa_remote: blacklist for ubu1 expired (302s old), retrying
session_evidence_2026_04_26.interpretation: exact 300s expiry; same RAM check repeated; user must wait full 5min for next probe

concrete_failures.issue: 300s TTL is fixed regardless of failure cause
concrete_failures.case_a: transient SSH timeout: 300s lockout is excessive (10s would suffice)
concrete_failures.case_b: persistent host down: 300s is too short, retries every 5min wasted
concrete_failures.case_c: RAM-insufficient temporary: 300s blocks a host that may free RAM in 30s

concrete_failures.issue: 24576MB threshold is fixed for 'large' workload class
concrete_failures.case_b: no graceful degradation: small workload could fit in 23934MB but is locked out anyway

concrete_failures.issue: PSI threshold absent in remote selection
concrete_failures.missing: process pressure (PSI some avg10) used for ranking but not for hard reject; high PSI hosts preferred if RAM OK
concrete_failures.session_evidence: selected hetzner with psi=89.62 (very high, near saturation)

remediation_options.implementation: track failure count per host in /tmp/hexa_remote.failcount.<host>
remediation_options.benefit: transient failures recover faster; persistent ones aren't retried as often

remediation_options.cost: medium — track reason code in blacklist file

remediation_options.approach: adaptive RAM threshold: dynamic based on actual workload measured
remediation_options.implementation: first run profiles peak RSS; cache as workload_class.ram_actual; future selection uses learned value × 1.2 buffer
remediation_options.risk: medium — first runs still use static threshold

remediation_options.approach: PSI hard-reject: if psi>80, reject regardless of RAM avail
remediation_options.risk: low — already gathered, just unused for reject
remediation_options.benefit: avoids running on near-saturated hosts (current pick had psi=89)

verdict: POLICY_RIGIDITY severity=MEDIUM; many places of 300s and threshold magic numbers; behavior degrades silently when host is borderline; adaptive backoff and PSI hard-reject are low-cost wins
